tagline: Calculate sand volume, weight, and bags for any project
title: Sand Calculator — How Much Sand Do I Need? (Tons, Yards & Bags)
metaDescription: Calculate tons, cubic yards, and bags of sand needed. Supports play sand, masonry sand, and fill sand with weight and cost estimates.
keywords: sand calculator, how much sand do I need, sand tonnage calculator, sandbox sand calculator, masonry sand calculator, sand volume calculator
calculatorHTML:
Sand Calculator

Quick Presets

Sandbox: 6-12" • Paver base: 1" • Fill/leveling: 2-4"
Bulk: $20-$45/ton • 50-lb bags: $4-$6 each
Cubic Yards
—
Tons
—

howItWorks.intro: Calculating sand is a volume-to-weight conversion. Measure your area, choose a depth, and this calculator handles the rest — including weight by sand type.
rules: Volume: Area (sq ft) × Depth (inches) / 12 = cubic feet. Divide by 27 for cubic yards
Weight varies by type: play sand ~2,500 lbs/yd³, fill sand ~2,700 lbs/yd³, masonry sand ~2,800 lbs/yd³
A 50-lb bag of sand contains about 0.5 cubic feet. One cubic yard = approximately 54 bags of 50-lb sand
howItWorks.outro: For sandboxes, use clean play sand rated for children — it is washed and screened to remove dust and debris. For paver bases, use coarse masonry sand (concrete sand). Fill sand is the cheapest option for grading and leveling.

faq.question: How much sand do I need for a sandbox?
faq.answer: For a 10' x 10' sandbox at 8 inches deep, you need about 2.5 cubic yards or 3.1 tons of play sand. That is roughly 124 bags of 50-lb play sand. A 6' x 6' sandbox at 6 inches needs about 0.67 cubic yards — around 33 bags.

faq.question: How much does a cubic yard of sand weigh?
faq.answer: Dry sand weighs 2,500-2,800 lbs per cubic yard depending on the type. Play sand: ~2,500 lbs (1.25 tons). All-purpose: ~2,700 lbs (1.35 tons). Masonry sand: ~2,800 lbs (1.4 tons). Wet sand can weigh 20-30% more.

faq.question: How many 50-lb bags of sand are in a cubic yard?
faq.answer: Approximately 54 bags of 50-lb sand equal one cubic yard. A 50-lb bag contains about 0.5 cubic feet, and there are 27 cubic feet in a cubic yard (27 / 0.5 = 54). For large projects, bulk delivery is much cheaper.

faq.question: What kind of sand do I use under pavers?
faq.answer: Use coarse concrete sand (also called masonry sand or C-33 sand) for paver bedding. Apply a 1-inch layer over compacted gravel base. Do not use play sand or beach sand — they are too fine and will wash out. Some contractors prefer polymeric sand for joints.

faq.question: How deep should sand be in a sandbox?
faq.answer: A sandbox should have at least 6 inches of sand, ideally 8-12 inches for good play depth. For a fall zone under play equipment, you need 9-12 inches minimum per safety standards. Always use play-grade sand that is non-toxic and dust-free.

faq.question: How much does sand cost?
faq.answer: Bulk sand: $20-$45 per ton delivered. 50-lb bags: $4-$6 each ($160-$324 per ton). Play sand is typically the most expensive type. Delivery fees range from $50-$150 depending on distance and quantity. Bulk is 3-5x cheaper than bagged for large projects.

chart.subtitle: Based on all-purpose sand at 2,700 lbs/yd³.

sections.title: What Is the Sand Calculator?
sections.body: The Sand Calculator is a free online tool designed for contractors and DIY builders who need quick, accurate calculations in the construction and building space. By entering your area input method, length, width, you get instant results including cubic yards, tons. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting cubic yards right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In construction and building, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by contractors and DIY builders worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your projects with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your area input method and need to find the right cubic yards. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Area Input Method
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your area input method — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: length, width, diameter, total area, depth, sand type, price per ton. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your cubic yards, tons. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your area input method before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both cubic yards and tons — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to plan your projects.
If you're unsure about your price per ton, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
